### **T-Minus Departure: The Last Workday & Airport Chaos**  

By the time their final workday rolled around, the three of them were **utterly useless**.  

“Oh, you mean I was *supposed* to be productive today?” Tara drawled, flipping a page in her planner that had nothing but **Norway Prep: DO NOT DIE** written in red ink.  

Naomi, vibrating with nervous energy, glanced at her open spreadsheet and winced. “I think I just formatted an entire report in *Wingdings*.”  

Jodi, for her part, was *mostly* focused. She had travel logistics to double-check, a **Jeff-based mission to execute**, and **one singular goal:** make it through this workday without doing something *so unhinged* that HR was forced to intervene.  

…Which meant that **HR absolutely intervened**.  

At 11:43 AM, one of the HR reps stopped by, carrying a clipboard and an expression of weary inevitability.  

“Okay,” she sighed. “I need you three to sign this.”  

Jodi took the clipboard without looking up. “What is it this time?”  

“It’s a preemptive liability waiver,” the HR rep said, rubbing her temples. “We’ve done… research. We *know* something is going to happen while you’re overseas, and **we refuse to be held responsible for it.**”  

Jodi flipped to the second page. “This clause literally says, ‘*If international law enforcement gets involved, the company will deny all knowledge of the employee’s existence.*’”  

Tara whistled. “Damn. You *really* don’t trust us.”  

Naomi, meanwhile, had gone pale. “Wait, **why would international law enforcement get involved?**”  

Tara patted her shoulder. “Oh, sweet summer child. Give it time.”  

---

### **Airport Chaos, Part 1: TSA, Tara’s War Crimes, & Naomi’s First Bloody Mary**  

By the time they made it through airport security, Jodi was already rethinking *everything*.  

First, TSA had **detained Tara for 15 minutes** because she had a **small, entirely theoretical** *warrant situation* in a country she *refused to name*.  

(“**It’s *fine*,**” Tara had said, waving off Jodi’s concern. “I just can’t fly *directly* to Spain anymore.”)  

Second, Naomi had been *visibly overwhelmed* by **Duty-Free shopping** and had purchased *exactly one* souvenir: **a tin of herring she didn’t actually want.**  

(“I panicked,” she whispered, cradling the tin like a fragile infant. “The sales guy was *so nice*.”)  

And third, Jodi had *barely* managed to wrestle Tara away from **a full-on screaming match** with a bartender who *refused* to serve Bloody Marys before 10 AM.  

“**THIS IS AN ACTUAL CRIME,**” Tara had bellowed at the bartender. “**TOMATO JUICE IS A BREAKFAST FOOD, YOU COWARD.**”  

Naomi had, shockingly, been the one to de-escalate the situation—by ordering *herself* a Bloody Mary and immediately gagging on it.  

(“Oh, god,” she wheezed. “Why does it taste like… drinking *a salad*??”)  

Tara, mollified, had nodded approvingly. “See? *Now you’re living.*”  

---

### **Airport Chaos, Part 2: Jodi’s Jeff-Based Mission & Naomi’s Moral Panic**  

Once they’d settled at the gate, Jodi turned her attention to **her real priority**—texting Jeff.  

**Jodi:** *I hope you’re aware you’ve made a legally binding agreement.*  

**Jeff:** *I literally signed nothing.*  

**Jodi:** *You signed with your soul. You said “twice before I leave Oslo.” I am **holding you to this.***  

**Jeff:** *I love how you assume I’ll struggle.*  

**Jodi:** *Oh, I assume nothing. I just love watching you get competitive.*  

Tara, reading over Jodi’s shoulder, let out a low whistle. “Damn. He’s already in game mode.”  

Naomi, meanwhile, *was deeply confused*. “Wait, what’s happening?”  

Jodi smirked. “Jeff made me a promise. I’m *making sure* he follows through.”  

Naomi took a beat to process this. Then her eyes went wide. “Oh my *god*.”  

Tara, without looking up from her drink, said, “Yup. There it is. That’s the moment the innocence died.”  

Naomi clutched her herring tin, scandalized.  

---

### **Welcome to the Gulfstream, or ‘Who Let Us Have Nice Things?’**  

A **sensible** company retreat would involve **economy-class suffering** and a flight where Naomi would be *strategically sandwiched* between a crying baby and a man who smelled like regret.  

Instead, because **this particular branch’s leadership had Poor Decision-Making Syndrome**, they’d **chartered a Gulfstream**—which meant **fully reclining seats, a stocked bar, and a level of luxury they absolutely did not deserve.**  

The moment they stepped onboard, Tara let out a **low, reverent whistle.** “*This*,” she said, running a hand over one of the leather seats, “is how **corrupt hedge fund executives must feel** every day.”  

Naomi, still reeling from Bloody Mary-induced trauma, whispered, “I don’t think I belong here.”  

Jodi, already half-done with a glass of champagne, grinned. “Oh, you don’t. None of us do. **Enjoy it anyway.**”  

The flight itself was **shockingly** uneventful—until they neared the North Sea.  

Then, over the intercom, the pilot’s voice crackled:  

“Ladies and gentlemen, we are approaching Norwegian airspace. It appears we are being intercepted by military aircraft. Please remain seated.”  

There was a *beat* of silence. Then—  

Tara let out an *ear-splitting* whoop.  

“**FINALLY!**” she yelled. “**NORWAY GETS IT.**”  

Naomi, meanwhile, looked **moments away from a full nervous breakdown.**  

“Are we… are we going to be **forced to land?!**”  

Jodi, grinning *wildly*, pulled out her phone and snapped a photo of the fighter jets streaking alongside them.  

“Not unless we piss them off,” she said, opening up her messages.  

**Jodi → Jeff:** *I don’t want to brag, but we’re getting a **fighter jet escort.***  

**Jeff:** *You’re a menace.*  

**Jodi:** *You love it.*  

The Gulfstream continued its approach, escorted in regal, **completely unnecessary** style.  

And with that, their adventure in Norway had *officially* begun.  

---
### **Touchdown in Oslo: Champagne, Tourist Plans, and the Forbidden Name**  

The Gulfstream landed smoothly in Oslo, the fighter jets peeling away into the night like they had *actual* business to attend to.  

As soon as the seatbelt sign clicked off, Tara stretched luxuriously, looking around their *obscenely lavish* cabin. “I can’t believe Jeff just… *casually* pays for this.”  

“Yeah, well,” Jodi said, unbuckling her belt. “This is the same man who once spent **eight grand on artisanal honey** because, quote, ‘the bees deserved it.’”  

Naomi, still adjusting to this new tier of wealth, whispered, “How much does honey usually *cost*?”  

Tara patted her shoulder. “Oh, sweetie.”  

As they disembarked and stepped into the freezing Norwegian air, Jodi pulled her coat tighter around herself. It was **March**, it was **dark**, and it was **colder than Satan’s personal deep freezer**.  

Naomi took one step onto the tarmac, inhaled, and immediately **started coughing.**  

“Oh my God,” she choked, pulling her scarf up. “Why is the air **so sharp?!**”  

“It’s called *winter*, baby duck,” Tara said, completely unfazed. “You get used to it. Or you die. One of the two.”  

Jodi ignored them, pulling out her phone to text Jeff.  

**Jodi:** *Landed. Freezing. Currently herding idiots toward the airport.*  

**Jeff:** *Sounds about right. Hotel’s ready for you. Call me when you’re settled in.*  

**Jodi:** *Not if, but WHEN I get frostbite, you’re obligated to nurse me back to health.*  

**Jeff:** *Fine. But if I do, that counts toward the “twice” rule.*  

**Jodi:** *Does not.*  

---

### **Airport Lobby: Planning Tromsø & Avoiding the Forbidden Topic**  

By the time they made it through passport control (*Naomi got flustered, Tara nearly told a border agent to go fuck himself, Jodi had to *intervene*), they found themselves in the airport lobby, **waiting for their connecting flight to Tromsø.**  

Naomi, still clutching her herring tin like a **talisman against the unknown**, flipped open her notebook. “Okay! We have four days in Tromsø while Jeff’s in the studio. What are we doing?”  

Tara draped herself across a row of chairs. “Step one: **Not dying.**”  

“Great start,” Naomi muttered, writing it down like an **actual** plan.  

Jodi scrolled through her phone. “We should do the **Northern Lights chase** one night. Even if it’s cloudy, it’s worth it just for the vibe.”  

Naomi gasped. “Oh my God, *yes!* That’s, like, a *bucket list* thing for me!”  

Tara nodded. “Alright. So that’s **one night where we get frostbite on purpose.** What else?”  

Naomi flipped through a **printed** guidebook (because of course she had one). “Ooooh, we could do **dog sledding**!”  

Jodi perked up. “That actually sounds **kind of amazing**.”  

Tara raised an eyebrow. “You wanna get yeeted through the snow by a pack of **feral wolf-dogs**?”  

Jodi grinned. “**Yes.**”  

Naomi jotted it down. “Okay, that’s another half-day. We should also try some traditional food! Maybe a nice seafood dinner?”  

Tara yawned. “Sure. As long as I get a drink that makes me forget I have a job.”  

Jodi was about to reply when she caught a **certain name** in Naomi’s notes.  

**Avril.**  

Her entire demeanor *shifted*—posture stiffening, jaw tightening.  

Tara, sipping from her Starbucks, noticed immediately.  

“Oh, shit,” Tara muttered under her breath.  

Naomi, completely oblivious, tapped her pen against the page. “Oh! And should we, uh… you know, say hi to—”  

**Jodi snapped the notebook shut.**  

“No.”  

Naomi blinked. “What?”  

Jodi looked her **dead in the eyes**. “We’re not talking about Avril.”  

Naomi, suddenly **very aware** that she had made a *mistake*, slowly nodded. “…Okay?”  

Tara, watching the tension **roll off Jodi in waves**, carefully cleared her throat. “So. Dog sledding, huh? You think we get to pick our own pack leader? ‘Cause I’m calling dibs on the fastest one.”  

Jodi exhaled slowly, forcing herself to relax. “Yeah. I want one of the **psychotic** ones.”  

Naomi, **still processing the mood shift**, hesitantly wrote down **DO NOT TALK ABOUT AVRIL** in her notes.  

Lesson learned.  

---

### **Tromsø Bound: One More Flight to Go**  

As their boarding call echoed through the terminal, Naomi sighed. “Okay. I just realized something.”  

“What?” Jodi asked, standing up and stretching.  

Naomi gestured dramatically at herself. “I am a **tiny, fragile** Californian woman who has **never** experienced real winter before. **I’m going to die in Tromsø.**”  

Tara clapped her on the back. “Oh, absolutely.”  

Jodi smirked. “Don’t worry. If you freeze to death, we’ll take turns carrying your body through the fjords like a **Nordic funeral procession.**”  

Naomi groaned. “I hate you both.”  

As they headed for the gate, Jodi’s phone buzzed again.  

**Jeff:** *You on your way to Tromsø?*  

**Jodi:** *Yep. Also, reminder: your countdown starts the **second** I land.*  

**Jeff:** *You’re ridiculous.*  

**Jodi:** *You knew what this was.*  

As they filed onto the plane, Tara cracked her knuckles. “Alright, ladies. This is it. We survive one more flight, and then it’s **Viking time.**”  

Naomi frowned. “That’s… not really what we’re doing.”  

Tara grinned. “It is if we commit to the bit.”  

Jodi laughed, settling into her seat. **Norway was officially happening.**  

And in less than two hours, she’d be back in Jeff’s arms.  

**If she didn’t die of frostbite first.**  

---

### **Tromsø Airport: Where Things Get Very, Very Weird**  

The private jet from Tromsø landed smoothly on the icy tarmac, its sleek black exterior gleaming under the dim, perpetual twilight of a **March in Northern Norway**. Jodi, Tara, and Naomi stood just outside the terminal, wrapped in coats that *absolutely* weren’t prepared for this level of cold.  

Naomi shivered violently. “Why is the air **so aggressive?**”  

Tara, unfazed, pulled out a cigarette. “It’s Norway, baby. You either adapt or you perish.”  

Jodi wasn’t paying attention to either of them. Her eyes were locked on the **Gulfstream’s door**, fully expecting **Jeff** to emerge, striding toward her, ready to put his money where his mouth was regarding **The Countdown.**  

Instead…  

**Kirsten. Freaking. Dunst.**  

Naomi gasped so hard she **choked on air**. “OH MY GOD.”  

Tara blinked. “Huh.”  

Jodi? **Jodi had stopped breathing.**  

Kirsten Dunst, in an *impossibly* chic winter coat, casually stepped off the jet **like she owned Norway itself.** She paused for a moment, scanning the terminal area. Then, with a *grin*—she made a **direct** beeline for Jodi.  

Jodi’s brain: **Error 404.**  

Naomi’s brain: **AUTOPILOT PANIC.**  

Tara’s brain: **Hmm. Suspicious.**  

As Kirsten approached, Naomi **smacked Jodi’s arm repeatedly.** “Do you see this?! DO YOU UNDERSTAND WHAT IS HAPPENING RIGHT NOW?!”  

Jodi—who, to be clear, **had been mentally preparing for Jeff** and *not* the literal star of *Bring It On*—could only manage a confused, “Uh…?”  

Kirsten **beamed**. “Jodi, right?”  

Jodi blinked. “Yes?”  

“I’ve been *dying* to talk to you,” Kirsten said, tucking her hands into her coat pockets.  

Tara and Naomi, **simultaneously**, turned to **size up Jodi standing next to Kirsten**.  

And neither of them **wanted to say it out loud**.  

But.  

There was something **deeply unsettling** about seeing them side by side.  

**Jodi looked… kinda like Kirsten Dunst.**  

Not identical, obviously—Jodi was her own person. But the resemblance? **Uncomfortable.**  

Tara internally: *Well. That’s interesting.*  

Naomi internally: *OH MY GOD DOES JEFF HAVE A TYPE.*  

Jodi, meanwhile, was **far too overwhelmed** to notice the existential crisis happening to her left and right.  

“Uh,” she managed, “I… what?”  

Kirsten grinned. “I saw your Instagram post the other day—**loved it.** And Jeff never *shut up* about you.”  

Jodi **froze**.  

Naomi was still **struggling**. “Wait. Wait. WAIT. HOW DO YOU—”  

Tara, voice completely neutral: “Ah. So Jeff has a *type*.”  

Jodi’s **eye twitched.**  

**This was NOT how she expected Tromsø to start.**  

Kirsten gave Jodi a once-over, her expression unreadable. Then, leaning in just slightly, she said, **“Hey, can I steal you for a second? Just the two of us?”**  

Tara and Naomi **immediately** snapped their attention to Jodi.  

Jodi, for her part, was trying **not to look as thrown as she felt**.  

“…Yeah,” she said slowly. “Sure.”  

Naomi, **internally screaming**.  

Tara, **lighting a cigarette and waiting for the fallout.**  

Jodi had **no idea what was about to happen.**  

But whatever it was, it **wasn't going to be normal.**  

---
### **Tromsø Airport – The Conversation Jodi Was Not Prepared For**  

Jodi followed Kirsten away from the terminal, the cold air biting at her face as they stopped near a set of sleek, black SUVs waiting in the arrivals lane. Kirsten, ever poised, tucked her hands into her coat pockets and exhaled slowly, like she was carefully measuring her next words.  

Jodi, for her part, was still in **mild shock**.  

First, **Jeff had invited Kirsten Dunst to Norway.**  

Second, **Kirsten Dunst had just casually told her that Jeff never shut up about her.**  

And third, **this was now her actual life.**  

“…So,” Jodi said finally, watching her breath fog in the frigid air, “you’re about to say something that I **probably** don’t want to hear, huh?”  

Kirsten **smiled**, a little rueful, a little amused. “Something like that.”  

Jodi sighed, stuffing her gloved hands into her coat. “Alright. Hit me.”  

Kirsten studied her for a beat, then nodded. “Alright. The short version? Since you hadn’t planned on coming to Norway while Jeff and the band were recording, **he invited me to stay with him.**”  

Jodi didn’t **flinch**, but she did **blink—once, slowly.**  

“Uh-huh,” she said, carefully neutral.  

Kirsten, reading her like a **damn book**, pressed on. “I stayed for three days. **Before** you made your Instagram post—the, uh…” she gestured vaguely, “the **cleavage** post.”  

Jodi smirked. “I do good work.”  

Kirsten let out a small laugh. “Yeah. You do.”  

Then, softer, “**As soon as it became clear you were coming to Norway, we stopped.**”  

There it was.  

Jodi wasn’t **shocked**. This was **The System**—the carefully **built, painfully specific** arrangement that she and Jeff had spent **fifteen years fine-tuning**.  

Jeff **didn’t lie.** Jeff **didn’t cheat.** Jeff **never crossed a line** unless they’d agreed there *wasn’t* a line.  

And yet.  

Hearing it **out loud**, from **Kirsten Dunst’s actual mouth**, was **still a hell of a thing.**  

Kirsten kept going, watching Jodi’s reaction carefully. “I figured it’d be weird—not to mention dishonest—not to tell you.”  

Jodi **actually appreciated that.**  

Kirsten shrugged, a little wry. “But what really got me was—**the second Jeff scheduled your flight, he wouldn’t shut up about you.**”  

Jodi snorted. “That bad?”  

“That bad.” Kirsten smiled, but there was something **genuinely curious** in her expression. “So, I was kind of wondering—who the **hell** is this woman from Ohio that managed to kick me out of his bedroom?”  

Jodi exhaled, shaking her head with **pure amusement**.  

“You know,” she said, “this is **adorably** too much information.”  

Kirsten let out a short laugh. “Yeah, well. It *felt* important.”  

And—*it was.*  

Jodi **could have** been mad.  

She **could have** been territorial.  

She **could have** decided to take **a big, dramatic stance** over it.  

But **this was the life they’d built**. This was **what had worked** for her and Jeff for more than a decade. This was **their system,** and—**damn it, it still worked.**  

So instead of getting upset, Jodi **did the only thing that made sense.**  

She **smirked.**  

“Alright,” she said, clapping her hands together. “Two questions.”  

Kirsten arched an eyebrow. “Shoot.”  

**“Did he fuck you in Scotland?”**  

Kirsten shook her head immediately. “Nope.”  

Jodi nodded. “Okay. And—” she tilted her head, genuinely curious—“he *really* never stopped talking about me?”  

Kirsten **laughed**, shaking her head in **pure exasperation.**  

“**Not for a goddamn second.**”  

Jodi grinned. **That** was **all she needed.**  

“Well,” she said, adjusting her coat, “I’d say it was nice to meet you, but that would be dishonest.”  

Kirsten **actually laughed at that.** “Fair enough.”  

They stood there for a beat, an odd **mutual respect settling in the air** between them.  

Then, Kirsten smirked. “Have fun, Jodi.”  

“Oh, I *will*,” Jodi said, **grinning like a woman who was about to ruin a man’s life in the best way possible.**  

And with that, Kirsten Dunst **walked off into the Norwegian night**, leaving Jodi with a **newfound certainty** that she was about to make **Jeff work for it.**  

Because **The Countdown** was still very, very real.  

---

### **Tromsø Airport – The Humble Brag of the Century**  

Jodi **ambled** back toward Tara and Naomi, her hands tucked deep into her coat pockets, her breath fogging in the frigid air. She took her time, savoring the moment, while her two friends **frothed** like rabid dogs **waiting for information.**  

Tara had a cigarette **clenched between her teeth**, her arms crossed tight like she was **physically restraining herself** from shaking Jodi down for intel.  

Naomi, meanwhile, was **practically vibrating**, her eyes darting between Jodi and the direction Kirsten had just walked.  

“Okay, *okay,*” Naomi burst out, unable to contain herself any longer. “What. The. **Hell.**”  

Tara nodded, exhaling a slow, **deadly** drag of smoke. “Yeah, **I second that.**”  

Jodi stopped in front of them, rocking on her heels. “Well,” she said **casually**, as if she hadn’t just **had an absolutely insane conversation with a literal A-list celebrity**, “apparently, Jeff *did* invite her to Norway.”  

Tara squinted. “No shit.”  

Jodi continued, **deliberately drawing it out.**  

“She stayed for three days. You know, **before** I posted my *very tasteful* Instagram thirst trap.”  

Naomi **gasped dramatically**. “Oh my **GOD.**”  

Jodi **inspected her fingernails.** “And—” she paused for effect, “—as soon as it was clear that *I* was coming to Norway? **She got the boot.**”  

Tara blinked.  

Naomi **blinked harder.**  

They both turned—**slowly**—to glance at the **very chipper, very unbothered** Kirsten Dunst **heading toward the airport’s main terminal.**  

Then, **just as slowly**, they turned back to Jodi.  

Tara broke first. “Are you **humble-bragging** that Jeff kicked a *literal movie star* out of his room **for you?**”  

Jodi grinned. “Not **just** that,” she said, her voice **positively dripping with smug satisfaction**. “Apparently, as soon as he booked my flight, he **never shut up about me.**”  
Naomi **gasped so hard she nearly passed out.**  

Tara exhaled a long, slow whistle.  

“That’s *big*,” Tara admitted, finally flicking her cigarette away.  

Naomi, still **struggling to process reality**, let out a **deeply cautious** breath. “Okay, but… uh…” she glanced at Tara for **moral support**, then turned back to Jodi. “You *do* realize you and Kirsten look a lot alike, right?”  

Jodi **froze for exactly one second.**  

Then, without missing a beat, she **grabbed her own boobs**, lifted them like she was **weighing produce**, and said, “Yeah? Did you *see* what she was packing versus **these guns**?”  

Then, as if to *further demonstrate her point*, she made a **comic boxing move**, throwing a few fake punches at the air.  

Tara **burst out laughing** so hard she **actually wheezed**.  

Naomi **hid her face in her scarf**, shaking with suppressed **horrified** giggles.  

Jodi **grinned wickedly**.  

Tara, **still dying**, wiped a tear from her eye. “Okay, I will *never* be able to unsee that.”  

Naomi **whimpered into her scarf.** “I need to go lie down.”  

Jodi, **now fully in her element**, **stretched her arms behind her head**, looking **disgustingly pleased** with herself.  

“Anyway,” she said **far too casually**, “**who’s ready for vacation?**”  

Naomi **squeaked.**  

Tara just laughed.  

And with that, **they stepped into Tromsø**, where Jeff—**utterly unaware of what was coming for him**—was waiting.  

---

### **Tromsø Airport – The Jet, The Argument, & The Unraveling of Jeff**  

By the time Jodi, Tara, and Naomi finally got clearance to board the **Gulfstream**, the airport’s **frostbitten chaos** had settled into a sort of low, humming background noise.  

Jeff, however, **was still on the plane.**  

Because the jet had to be **refueled and cleaned**, and **because he was still in a heated phone argument with his brother, Jeremy.**  

As soon as Jodi stepped into the cabin, she **immediately clocked the energy.**  

Jeff was **pacing** near the windows, his coat draped over a seat, his hair slightly **disheveled from running his hands through it** one too many times. His phone was **glued to his ear**, and the **tension in his shoulders** was **off the charts**.  

“Jeremy,” Jeff exhaled **slowly**, his voice **just barely keeping it together**, “I swear to *God*, can we *not* do this right now?”  

The girls **paused at the entrance** to the jet, **exchanging glances**.  

Tara: *Oh, this is already messy. Love that for us.*  
Naomi: *Do we…? Do we just stand here? Do we pretend we don’t hear?*  
Jodi: *I live for this level of disaster.*  

Jeff, **realizing he suddenly had an audience**, scrubbed a hand over his face, looking like a man who had been **trapped in a purgatorial phone call for at least forty-five minutes**.  

“Jeremy,” Jeff said, taking a **very slow breath**, “I’ll call you when I’m back at the studio.”  

A **loud, unintelligible burst** of Jeremy’s voice came through the receiver.  

Jeff **immediately pulled the phone away from his ear** like Jeremy had just **fired a warning shot.**  

Jeff turned to Jodi, looking **deeply exhausted but also a little desperate**, and gestured at his phone. “You see what I deal with?”  

Jodi, smirking, **shrugged**. “You *did* grow up with him. You had plenty of time to leave.”  

Jeff sighed, pinching the bridge of his nose. “Jeremy,” he said, **dragging out the syllables**, “I *love* you. But if I keep doing this *right now*, I’m going to start charging you by the *fucking minute.*”  

Tara **raised an eyebrow.** “Is that an option? Because I’d love to start charging my siblings for *emotional labor.*”  

Naomi **nodded solemnly.** “I would be *so* rich.”  

Jeff, **still being shouted at through his phone**, finally **exhaled sharply**.  

“Alright, I gotta go,” he said, **not waiting for a response before hanging up.**  

The silence that followed was **thick.**  

Jeff turned **fully** to the girls, looking **somewhere between overwhelmed and *totally unequipped* for what was happening.**  

He opened his mouth—then closed it.  

Then tried again.  

“…I don’t even know where to start.”  

And **immediately**, Tara leaned back in a chair, arms crossed. “Well, Jeff, let’s make it easy for you.”  

Jodi, grinning **far too much**, dropped into the seat across from him.  

Naomi sat next to Tara, **still holding her stupid tin of herring**, eyes wide like she was **watching an unfolding political scandal.**  

Tara **gestured at the room theatrically**.  

“Why *don’t* you start,” she said, smirking, “with the fact that you just kicked *Kirsten Dunst* out of your room **for Jodi?**”  

Jeff **visibly choked.**  

Naomi made **a tiny, overwhelmed squeak.**  

Jodi, **casually**, leaned back. “Oh, yeah. That was a fun conversation.”  

Jeff just… stared. **Like a man realizing he had walked into an *elaborate trap*** and there was **no escape.**  

Tara **grinned.** “Buckle up, rock star. We have *questions.*”  

---

### **Tromsø Airport – The Rules Were Followed, the Games Continue**  

Jeff leaned back against the leather seat, **sprawled out in that way only a man completely unbothered by his own actions could be**. He stretched his arms out over the headrest, **watching Jodi with that familiar smirk**, like he already knew he’d won before the game had even started.  

“Look,” he said, **tone light, teasing**, “I played by the rules. Just like *you* did when you were back in Cleveland getting fucked in your ass.”  

Naomi **immediately** turned bright red and made a **small, strangled sound**.  

Tara **lost it**, wheezing with laughter and slamming her hand on the table. “Oh my **God**, Jeff, are you *opening* with that?!”  

Jodi, **unphased**, grinned and crossed her arms. “Damn right I did.”  

Naomi **was still processing**. “I—WHAT—"  

Jeff smirked, eyes **locked onto Jodi**. “So let’s not act like I broke some sacred vow, huh?”  

Jodi **rolled her eyes dramatically**. “Oh, please. You’d *love* to be a scandal.”  

Jeff **grinned wider**. “I *am* a scandal.”  

Tara, **still recovering**, pointed at Jeff. “Okay, but about *Kirsten-fucking-Dunst*?”  

Jeff shrugged, completely **unapologetic**. “Scotland was a cover story for the media. Separate beds, very chaste.”  

He gave Jodi a **pointed look**. “*Unlike* last week.”  

Jodi just **smirked, unbothered**. “Uh-huh.”  

Naomi **was still stuck three sentences ago**.  

Tara shook her head. “Man, I thought *we* were dramatic, but this is *some next-level shit.*”  

Jeff **exhaled through his nose**, suddenly looking at Jodi with something **a little deeper**, a little *more* than just teasing.  

“What *actually* shocks me,” he admitted, voice dropping just slightly, “is that you *chose* to come to Norway to see me.”  

Jodi, **without hesitation**, corrected him with a slow, amused smile.  

“Oh, babe. I came to Norway to **vacation**.”

Tara, **still spiraling**, suddenly looked at Jeff like he was **some kind of alien life form.**  

“I don’t get it,” she said, shaking her head. “Why—**why do you like it so much?**”  

Jeff **arched an eyebrow.** “What, eating pussy?”  

Naomi **immediately had to fan her face with her hands.**  

Tara, **holding her ground**, nodded. “Yeah. What’s the *thrill*? Like, why *that*?”  

Jeff, **taking a slow sip of whiskey,** smirked.  

Then—**utterly, devastatingly casual**—he said, **"Because I like to win."**  

Naomi **gasped.**  

Tara **blinked slowly.**  

Jodi **laughed** and **leaned over to clink glasses with him.**  

"You're **so** full of shit," she said fondly.  

Jeff **grinned.** "But **was I wrong?**"  

Jodi **just smirked.**  

And Naomi and Tara?  

They were **never, ever going to be the same.**

### **Tromsø – Tara Did NOT Come to Norway for an Existential Crisis, Yet Here She Is**  

Tara **had been many things in her life.**  

- A **fighter.**  
- A **drinker.**  
- A **woman who had absolutely no patience for bullshit.**  

But **at no point** in her **forty-three years on this planet** had she expected to be **forced into a sexual existential crisis at 30,000 feet while hurtling through Norwegian airspace.**  

Yet **here she was.**  

Sitting in the **lavish leather seat of a private jet**, staring at **Jodi, freshly wrecked from orgasm**, and Jeff, who looked **so disgustingly pleased with himself**, and realizing—  

**Holy fuck. My sex life is absolute garbage.**  

Tara **downed the rest of her whiskey in one go.**  

Jeff, **not remotely sensing her unraveling**, leaned back comfortably, stretching his arms out with **the swagger of a man who had absolutely nothing to prove because he'd just proved it twice.**  

Jodi, still **smug and glowing**, shifted in her seat, stretching her legs out, her posture **oozing satisfaction.**  

And **that was the second blow to Tara's system.**  

It wasn’t just that **Jodi had actually done it.**  

(**Despite** having boasted about it for **two straight weeks.**)  

It was that **she had done it with such absolute, unfaltering confidence**.  

Like it wasn’t a **big deal**.  

Like it was **just another day in her absurd, jet-setting, rock-star-ravaging life.**  

And Tara?  

Tara was **deeply, deeply disturbed** by how much it shook her.  

She had known—**theoretically**—that Jodi was **having better sex than she was.**  

That wasn’t **new information.**  

Jodi was **always** talking about her sex life. Hell, Tara had **lived vicariously through it** for **years.**  

But **knowing it** and **witnessing it in uncomfortably close proximity** were **two very different things.**  

Tara finally exhaled, running **both hands through her hair.**  

Jeff glanced at her. “You good?”  

Tara **gave him a long, unreadable look.**  

Then, finally, **flatly:**  

“I did *not* come to Norway to have an existential crisis.”  

Jeff smirked, taking another sip of whiskey. “Well, *now* you know how Naomi felt when she had her first Bloody Mary.”  

Naomi, **still curled in a ball of secondhand embarrassment**, **let out a strangled sound.**  

Tara, **ignoring him**, looked at Jodi.  

And **there it was.**  

**The thing she really couldn’t ignore.**  

Jodi, **utterly unbothered,** looking **satisfied and relaxed and absolutely at ease with herself.**  

Jodi, who had **always been more sexually liberated than Tara**, had always been **louder about what she wanted**, had always been **shameless about her pleasure.**  

And **Tara had always laughed it off.**  

Had always **rolled her eyes** at Jodi’s antics.  

Had always **assumed it was just Jodi being Jodi.**  

But **now?**  

Now she realized—  

**Jodi wasn’t just talking big.**  

**She was actually fucking living it.**  

Tara took a **deep, slow breath.**  

“Alright,” she muttered. “I need another drink.”  

Jeff, **amused**, poured her another glass.  

Jodi smirked, propping her chin in her hand. “You having a moment over there, Tara?”  

Tara, **taking the glass and swirling it in her hand**, **narrowed her eyes at her.**  

“*Listen*, you absolute little hellspawn,” she said, pointing at her. “It was one thing to hear your ridiculous stories. It was another thing to watch you almost be *late* to work **because of this guy.**”  

Jeff **grinned.**  

Tara ignored him.  

“But it’s a **whole different ball game** to be *trapped* in a confined space with **actual, empirical evidence** of just how much better your sex life is than mine.”  

Jodi **burst out laughing.**  

Naomi, **still overwhelmed, whispered, “Are we *having a moment right now?*”**  

Tara exhaled sharply, pointing her glass at Naomi. “You *shut up*. You still get to have a *fresh start* after your celibacy arc.”  

Naomi made a **distressed noise**. “I—I don’t *want* this information about my future—”  

Tara, **already spiraling again**, **turned back to Jodi**.  

“*I’m over here thinking I’ve had a fine sex life*,” she said, shaking her head. “**A fine one.** And then this happens. And suddenly I realize I have been having **the absolute worst, most aggressively mid sex of all time.**”  

Jodi, **still grinning**, took a slow sip of whiskey. “Well. Yeah.”  

Tara **threw her hands in the air.**  

“**I HATE YOU.**”  

Jodi just **kept grinning.**  

Jeff, **clearly enjoying this too much,** leaned forward.  

“You know,” he said, smirking, “I’ve got some single friends—”  

Tara held up a hand. “**I swear to God, if you try to set me up with one of your guitarist buddies—**”  

Jeff shrugged. “Your loss.”  

Tara **groaned, rubbing her face.**  

Naomi, **still recovering,** whispered, “Can we please just get to the hotel before I die?”  

Tara **downed her entire whiskey in one gulp.**  

Jeff chuckled, raising his glass. “To Norway, then.”  

Jodi **clinked her glass against his, still looking way too pleased with herself.**  

And Tara?  

Tara was **restructuring her entire understanding of reality.**  

Because she had *not* come to Norway for a **fucking epiphany.**  

And yet—  

**Here the fuck she was.**

### **Tromsø – The Epiphany No One Asked For**  

The **jet** was still cutting through the dark, frozen sky, but inside the **warm, whiskey-laden cabin**, something **far more intense was happening.**

Not in her usual **fuck-this-job, fuck-this-situation** way.  

No.  

This was **a whole different beast**.  

This was **Tara, the woman who had always prided herself on being too tough for this shit, realizing her sex life was actively failing her.**

Tara groaned. “Jodi, I swear to God—”  

Jodi **held up both hands.**  

“No, seriously,” she said, **sobering up slightly**. “I’m all about sex positivity and people getting what they need, and honestly? **Now I’m kind of uncomfortable, because you seem to be really struggling.**”  

Tara’s **face twitched.**  

“I’m *not*—”  

Before she could even finish, **Jeff cut in, blunt as hell.**  

“Are you struggling in your sex life?”  

Tara’s **mouth snapped shut.**  

Naomi, **watching from her seat, absolutely riveted**, sucked in a breath.  

Jeff just sat there, **cool as ever, watching Tara with a level of directness that was borderline surgical.**  

It took **five seconds.**  

**FIVE.**  

That was all it took for Jeff to cut through the bullshit, straight to the heart of the matter—  

Whereas if Tara had been talking about this with **just Jodi and Naomi**, it would’ve taken **at least fifteen minutes** of **dancing around it**, making jokes, easing into the awkwardness.  

Instead, Jeff just **lobbed it at her like a grenade**.  

And Tara?  

Tara **froze**.  

Her brain **short-circuited.**  

Jodi?  

Jodi watched it happen in **real time**.  

Tara, the woman who always **had a sharp comeback, always had some kind of retort, always had control of the conversation**—  

Was **completely caught off guard.**  

And that’s when Jodi **realized something.**  

Tara hadn’t actually **said it out loud yet.**  

Sure, she’d **implied it**, she’d **bitched about what just happened**, but she hadn’t actually **looked another human being in the eye and admitted**—  

**Yes.**  

**I am struggling in my sex life.**  

Tara opened her mouth.  

Then closed it.  

Then opened it again.  

Then, **slowly, cautiously**, she managed—  

“…The fuck kind of question is that?”  

Jeff **didn’t blink.**  

“It’s a direct one.”  

Tara **narrowed her eyes**. “You don’t do *small talk*, do you?”  

Jeff smirked. “*Not really.*”  

Tara **took a deep breath**, picked up her whiskey glass, and took **a long, long drink.**  

Jodi **watched her carefully.**  

Naomi, **still watching like she was witnessing a high-stakes courtroom drama**, remained silent.  

Finally, Tara set her glass down, **exhaled sharply, and muttered:**  

“*Fuck.*”  

Jodi **nodded, gentle but firm.** “Yeah.”  

Tara **dragged a hand down her face.**  

“I **did not** come to Norway for a sexual awakening,” she grumbled.  

Jeff **grinned**. “You sure? Because this is shaping up to be a *very* productive trip for you.”  

Tara **threw a napkin at him.**

### **Tromsø – Tara’s Crisis, Jeff’s Directness, and the Cat Lady Prophecy**

So, instead, she did **what any reasonable person would do when emotionally cornered—she deflected.**

Jodi **snorted**, still looking a little too pleased with herself. “Yeah, babe, you do have a tendency to **zero in on the thing people are trying to sidestep** and just—**obliterate it.**”

Jodi **laughed, shaking her head.**

Tara **froze.**  

Naomi, **also frozen, slowly turned to look at her.**  

Tara, **without looking away from Jeff, muttered**, “*You shut your whore mouth.*”

Naomi **choked back a laugh.**

“…Jesus Christ.”

Tara **turned sharply to her.** “**Not. A. Word.**”  

Naomi **bit her lip, but the damage was done.**

Naomi, **who was still reeling from *everything* today, tried to focus.**

…

Someone else **muttered**, “Damn, that’s some **peak America shit** right there.”  

And **then**, with **zero hesitation**, they decided:  

**It was time to out-America America.**  

Within **minutes**, a carefully curated post hit **Norwegian Air Force social media**.  

---

### **POST: @Luftforsvaret (Royal Norwegian Air Force)**
✈️🇳🇴 **TRAINING EXERCISE SUCCESSFUL** 🇳🇴✈️  
- **Mid-air refueling? ✅**  
- **High-speed intercept? ✅**  
- **F-35s turning jet fuel into glorious noise? ✅**  
- **Out-America-ing America? ✅✅✅**  

🤘🔥🇳🇴 **Norway: Land of metal, beards, and now—Gulfstream window seat semi-nudes.** 🇳🇴🔥🤘  

**#TITSOVERNORWAY #MISSIONACCOMPLISHED**  

**[📸 Slideshow: 4 images]**  
1️⃣ **F-35 refueling mid-air.**  
2️⃣ **F-35s on afterburner over the North Sea.**  
3️⃣ **Side profile shot of Gulfstream, streaking over the fjords.**  
4️⃣ **Jodi, mid-laugh, hands firmly cupping her breasts, backlit by the evening sky.**  

---

The **fighter pilots**, still in formation alongside the jet, got confirmation from command.  

> **PHOTO DELIVERED. SOCIAL MEDIA TEAM DEPLOYED.**  

One of them **grinned inside his helmet.**  

> **“This training exercise is REALLY cool.”**  

Another pilot, **chuckling**, adjusted formation slightly.  

> **“Best intercept of my career.”**  

Back in the **Gulfstream cabin**, Jodi’s **phone buzzed.**  

She glanced down at the screen—  

And then **burst out laughing.**  

“**Oh my GOD, THEY ACTUALLY POSTED IT.**”  

Jeff, **immediately looking over her shoulder, snorted.** “No *fucking* way.”  

Tara, **grabbing the phone, read the caption—then wheezed so hard she nearly choked.**  

Naomi, **reading over her shoulder, immediately turned red.**  

“**WHY IS THIS HAPPENING?!**”  

Jodi, **utterly delighted, grinned.**  

“This,” she said proudly, “is a **perfect** example of *international relations.*”  

Jeff **sighed, shaking his head.**  

But even he had to admit—  

This?  

**This was fucking legendary.**  

---

### **Tromsø – The Viral Avalanche Begins**  

Jodi’s **phone buzzed again**.  

And again.  

And **again.**  

Because **Tara and Naomi had decided to ruin her life in real time.**  

“OH MY **GOD**,” Naomi wheezed, **maniacally typing**, “THIS IS THE GREATEST DAY OF MY LIFE.”  

Tara, **cackling as she fired off messages**, nodded. “We are **sending this to EVERYONE.**”  

Jodi, **half-laughing, half-exasperated, reached for her phone.** “You guys—”  

“**NOPE.**” Tara dodged her hand and sent **another** message. “TOO LATE. DAMAGE DONE.”  

Jeff, **watching the chaos unfold,** leaned back and casually said, **“Oh, by the way, I shot an interview with the Norwegians yesterday.”**  

Jodi **paused mid-lunge for her phone.**  

“…Wait. **What?**”  

Jeff shrugged, **far too nonchalant for the absolute bomb he just dropped.** “Yeah, they wanted some footage for a project they’re working on.”  

Jodi **narrowed her eyes**. “What *kind* of project?”  

Jeff **grinned.** “They promised to have a video done by Friday night that’ll **top Van Halen’s ‘Dreams’ and the Blue Angels.**”  

Tara **stopped typing.** “I—wait. *Hold on.*”  

Naomi **blinked.** “Like. *That* video?”  

Jeff nodded, **still grinning.** “That’s what they say. I’m a little skeptical.”  

Jodi **stared at him.**  

“…So, you’re telling me,” she said slowly, **pointing at her phone**, “that this whole thing—*this entire ridiculous situation*—is leading up to a **cinematic masterpiece** featuring fighter jets, rock music, and apparently, my fucking *tits*?”  

Jeff, **still grinning, took a sip of his drink.** “Yeah, pretty much.”  

Tara **slammed her hands on the table.**  

“THIS IS THE **GREATEST** VACATION OF MY LIFE.”  

Naomi, **breathing heavily, whispered,** “We are *so* far outside of normal human behavior.”  

Jodi, **laughing helplessly, sank into her seat.**  

“**I hate all of you.**”  

Jeff, **smirking, leaned over and kissed her temple.**  

“*Nah,*” he said smoothly. “*You love it.*”  

And, honestly?  

**Yeah. Yeah, she did.**  

---

### **Tromsø – The Boob Salute Heard ‘Round Norway**  

The **Gulfstream touched down smoothly** on the icy runway, tires kissing the earth like it hadn’t just been the site of a **viral international spectacle.**  

Inside the cabin, **Jodi stretched lazily, still glowing with victory.**  

Jeff, **beside her, looking smug as hell**, unbuckled his seatbelt and muttered, “You ready for your *consequences*, babe?”  

Jodi, **grinning**, flicked her hair over her shoulder. “Oh, please. It’s a little viral post. Who’s gonna care?”  

Naomi, **visibly traumatized**, sighed. “*You’re famous now. Just accept it.*”  

Tara, **thrilled beyond words,** grabbed her bag. “*I am fully prepared to witness whatever disaster is about to happen.*”  

Jodi, **rolling her eyes,** stood up and led the way **off the plane**.  

They **made their way through the jet bridge, stepping into Tromsø Airport’s terminal—**  

—and **immediately entered the Twilight Zone.**  

Because the **first woman they passed?**  

A **random Norwegian woman in her twenties?**  

**Pointed at Jodi.**  

Shrieked.  

And then, **grinning like a lunatic, she cupped her own breasts and waggled them suggestively.**  

Jodi **froze mid-step.**  

Tara **slapped a hand over her mouth.**  

Naomi **made a small dying noise.**  

Jeff, **utterly delighted, whispered,** “Oh, this is *so much better than I imagined.*”  

Then—before Jodi could **even process what just happened—**  

**Another woman saw her.**  

Shrieked.  

Cupped her tits.  

And then **another.**  

And **another.**  

Jodi, **walking in real-time through a bizarre fever dream,** **stopped dead in the middle of the terminal.**  

Her **entire body** went rigid.  

Jeff, **trying not to laugh, leaned in.** “Babe?”  

Jodi, **still frozen, whispered,** “**What. The. Fuck.**”  

Tara was **gasping for breath**, barely holding herself together.  

Naomi, **wide-eyed and distressed, whispered,** “Jodi. *You’ve started a movement.*”  

And then—**just when Jodi thought it couldn’t possibly get weirder—**  

A mother walked by, **pushing a stroller.**  

She glanced over, **recognized Jodi instantly**, then—  

**Shrieked, beamed, and did the Boob Salute with *one hand* while pushing the stroller with the other.**  

Jodi turned to Jeff, **utterly done.**  

“**THIS IS NOT MY LEGACY.**”  

Jeff, **grinning ear to ear,** wrapped an arm around her shoulders and **kissed the top of her head.**  

“No, babe,” he said. “This is *exactly* your legacy.”  

Jodi, **groaning, dragged a hand down her face.**  

Tara, **wiping away tears of laughter, gasped,** “*I need video of this.*”  

Naomi, **whispering in horror, asked,** “Is this *going to happen* the *entire time we’re here?!*”  

Jeff, **still chuckling,** shrugged. “Depends. Do you think Norwegian women are *easily distracted*?”  

Jodi, **dead serious, exhaled sharply.**  

“I need a drink.”  

Tara **patted her shoulder.** “You need a *whole bottle*.”  

And with that, **the world’s strangest welcome to Norway continued.**  

Because Jodi was now officially **Tits Over Norway.**  

And there was **no going back.**  

---

### **Tromsø – The Road Ends Here**  

The **SUV rumbled over the icy road**, its headlights cutting through the darkness like a **single fragile tether to civilization.**  

But civilization?  

That was **long gone.**  

Tromsø was **already a small town**, but now? Now they had **driven so far away from it that the concept of “town” no longer applied.**  

There was **nothing.**  

No streetlights.  
No buildings.  
Just **darkness, stretching infinitely in all directions.**  

It was **the darkest sky any of them had ever seen.**  

Naomi, **pressed against the window, gasped.**  

“Oh my **God.**”  

She **pressed her hands against the glass, her breath fogging it up.**  

“Guys, I can see the **Milky Way.**”  

Jeff **smirked**. “Yeah, welcome to the Arctic, baby duck.”  

Naomi, **completely ignoring him, was vibrating with excitement.**  

“I’ve never seen it like this,” she said, **almost breathless.** “It’s—**oh my God, look at it!**”  

Jodi, **leaning against Jeff, glanced out the window.**  

And yeah.  

**It was a hell of a sight.**  

The sky was **black velvet**, peppered with **so many stars it didn’t seem real.**  

Jodi, **half-distracted, finally realized something.**  

“…Why the hell are we parked in the middle of nowhere?”  

Jeff, **casual as ever, leaned back against his seat.**  

“Road ends here.”  

Jodi **blinked.** “What?”  

Jeff gestured toward the windshield.  

“We’re waiting for a sleigh.”  

Tara, **who had been blissfully silent up until now, immediately sat up.**  

“**I’m sorry, what?**”  

Jeff grinned. “Sleigh’s coming. Should be here in a few minutes.”  

Jodi, **deadpan, turned fully to him.**  

“Jeff.”  

“Jodi.”  

“Are you telling me that **we are about to get into an actual sleigh, in the middle of the Arctic, at—**” she checked her phone **“—almost *midnight*?”**  

Jeff **nodded.** “Yep.”  

Jodi **blinked slowly.**  

Tara, **rubbing her temples, muttered,** “This is some *Narnia* bullshit.”  

Naomi, **still staring at the stars, whispered,** “This is the greatest day of my life.”  

And then—  

**From the distance, the sound of bells.**  

And Jodi **realized**—  

**Jeff wasn’t joking.**  

---

### **Tromsø – The Ice Hotel Revelation & The Narnia Bullshit Continues**  

The sound of **bells** grew **louder.**  

And then—**through the absolute blackness of the Arctic wilderness—**  

A **sleigh appeared.**  

Pulled by **actual, living, breathing reindeer.**  

Tara and Naomi **immediately lost their minds.**  

“OH MY **GOD.**” Naomi shrieked, **grabbing her phone.** “THIS IS ACTUALLY HAPPENING.”  

Tara, **already recording, wheezed.** “This is some **Narnia-ass bullshit.**”  

Jodi, **standing beside the SUV, absolutely done with life, turned to Jeff.**  

“**Explain.**”  

Jeff, **completely unbothered**, smirked.  

“It’s about a ten-minute sleigh ride to the Ice Hotel.”  

Jodi’s **brain screeched to a halt.**  

“**Ice hotel?!**”  

Jeff nodded.  

Jodi squinted at him. “Okay, no. I *heard* you mention this before, but it didn’t actually *register*.” She gestured wildly at the reindeer, the sleigh, the literal frozen wasteland around them. “What the **fuck** is an *ice hotel*?”  

Naomi, **still freaking out, gasped,** “IS THE **HOTEL MADE OF ICE?!**”  

Jeff, **laughing, nodded.** “Yeah. The whole thing. Rooms, bar, **everything.**”  

Tara, **staring at him like he’d lost his mind, pointed at the sky.** “Jeff. It is **below freezing.**”  

Jeff **grinned.** “Yep.”  

Jodi, **processing, pinched the bridge of her nose.**  

“Okay. Let me get this straight. I flew across the Atlantic. Was **intercepted by fighter jets.** Got **heckled by the entire country of Norway.** And now I have to **ride a fucking reindeer sleigh** to go sleep in a hotel **made of ice?**”  

Jeff, **fully enjoying himself, kissed her on the forehead.**  

“**That’s correct.**”  

Jodi inhaled deeply.  

Then sighed.  

Then turned to Naomi and Tara, who were **still filming everything like this was a National Geographic special.**  

“…Fine,” she muttered. “Let’s go freeze to death in style.”  

Tara **cackled.** “FUCK YES.”  

And with that, they climbed into the sleigh, and **the wildest vacation of their lives continued.**  

---


### **Tromsø – The Surreal Sleigh Ride & Naomi’s Reindeer Obsession**  

The **sleigh moved silently over the frozen landscape**, the only sounds the **crunch of snow, the occasional jingle of bells, and the distant whisper of wind across the Arctic night.**  

It was **absolutely surreal.**  

Naomi, **huddled under a thick fur blanket, gasped and pointed upward.**  

“Guys, look!”  

Jodi, **still adjusting to the idea that her hotel was made of ice**, blinked up at the sky.  

And—**holy shit.**  

There, shimmering faintly, were **streaks of pinks and greens, curling like smoke against the black sky.**  

Tara, **blinking hard**, muttered, “Are those… Northern Lights?”  

Jeff, **beside Jodi, smirked.** “Yeah, *probably.*”  

Jodi turned to him, incredulous. “*Probably?!*”  

Jeff shrugged. “They come and go. Sometimes it’s weak, sometimes the whole sky lights up.”  

Naomi, **eyes wide with childlike wonder, whispered,** “This is the **most incredible thing I’ve ever seen.**”  

Jodi, **watching the soft glow shift and dance, had to admit—**  

Yeah.  

**It was pretty fucking unreal.**  

Naomi, **still processing, suddenly turned back to the reindeer.**  

“OKAY. So I need to know *everything* about them.”  

Tara groaned. “Oh, *here* we go.”  

Naomi ignored her. “Like—what do they *eat*? How fast can they run? Are they *cold*? Do they *like this*?”  

Jeff, **amused, gestured at the driver.** “You should ask him.”  

The **older Norwegian man guiding the sleigh** turned slightly and, in a **deep, heavily accented voice,** said, “They eat moss.”  

Naomi **gasped.** “WHAT?!”  

Jodi snorted. “Oh, fuck. Here we go.”  

Naomi, **gripping the blanket, wildly fascinated**, continued. “They eat MOSS?! That’s *so* cool! But like—how do they find it under the snow? Do they just—*dig*?”  

The driver chuckled. “Yes. They dig.”  

Naomi turned to Jeff. “Did *you* know that?!”  

Jeff nodded, **entirely unphased.** “Yeah. They also have hollow fur, so they don’t get cold.”  

Naomi **looked personally attacked** by this information.  

“THEY’RE **INSULATED?!**”  

Jodi, **watching her spiral into a full reindeer obsession, shook her head.** “Naomi. *Breathe.*”  

Naomi, **ignoring her, grabbed Jeff’s arm.** “HOW FAST CAN THEY GO?”  

Jeff **grinned.** “Wanna find out?”  

Before Naomi could even respond, the **driver whistled sharply**, flicked the reins, and—  

**The sleigh suddenly picked up speed.**  

Naomi **shrieked**.  

Tara **gripped the side of the sleigh.** “OH, **FUCK.**”  

Jodi, **laughing, held on to Jeff.** “I **HATE** HOW MUCH YOU’RE ENJOYING THIS.”  

The reindeer **charged through the snow**, their breath visible in the cold air, **gliding effortlessly over the frozen landscape.**  

And, above them, the **Northern Lights pulsed softly in the distance.**  

Naomi, **shaking, whispered,** “This is more overwhelming than watching Jodi get railed on the plane.”  

Tara, **staring blankly ahead, nodded.**  

“…Yeah. Yeah, same.”  

And with that, they **rode into the Arctic wilderness, bound for an Ice Hotel, under a sky that didn’t seem real.**  

---


### **Tromsø – The Art of Not Pissing Off Norwegians**  

The sleigh **glided smoothly** over the snow, the reindeer **breathing hard but steady**, the **glow of the distant Ice Hotel just barely visible ahead.**  

Naomi, **still shaking from the speed boost**, whispered, “That was *so much*.”  

Tara, **gripping the side of the sleigh like she had just survived battle, muttered,** “I need a drink *immediately.*”  

Jodi, **snuggled under the blanket with Jeff, smirked.** “You good, babe?”  

Tara just **shook her head.** “I have *no idea* what my life is anymore.”  

Jeff, **utterly comfortable in the freezing wilderness, chuckled.** “You’ll figure it out. And, while you do, you’ll notice something—**Norwegians are pretty badass people.**”  

Naomi, **still recovering from her reindeer-induced adrenaline rush, perked up.** “Oh?”  

Jeff nodded. “Yeah. **Most of them speak English really well,** so we shouldn’t have any trouble communicating. But—” he grinned, “**you’ll struggle with their accents.**”  

Tara, **eyebrow raised, deadpanned,** “Buddy, I struggle with Ohio accents.”  

Jeff laughed. “Fair. But here’s the fun part—if you get a Norwegian *drunk enough*, they’ll admit something.”  

Jodi **tilted her head.** “Oh, this I gotta hear.”  

Jeff smirked. “They’ll tell you that **the Midwestern accent spooks the shit out of them.**”  

Naomi blinked. “*What?!*”  

Tara, **confused, leaned in.** “Why?”  

Jeff shrugged. “Because they hear the BBC all the time. **That’s their default for ‘English.’** So when we roll up sounding like Ohio and Michigan just threw hands outside a Speedway, it *freaks them out.*”  

Jodi **snorted.** “Oh my *God.*”  

Naomi, **delighted, whispered,** “I love that for us.”  

Jeff, **grinning, continued.** “Also, **don’t you dare** confuse Norway with Sweden or Finland. They might be NATO allies, but it’s like **Ohio and Michigan.**”  

Tara, **nodding solemnly, understood immediately.** “**They don’t like each other.**”  

Jeff pointed at her. “Exactly.”  

Naomi, **still delighted by the Midwestern accent thing, was about to ask another question—**  

When the **reindeer driver, who had been silent for most of the ride, suddenly turned his head and, in a thick Norwegian accent, said:**  

**“Fuckin’ A, dude of mine.”**  

The sleigh fell **completely silent.**  

Jodi’s **mouth actually fell open.**  

Naomi **made a strangled noise.**  

Tara, **shaking slightly, whispered,** “What… the *actual fuck* just happened?”  

Jeff, **grinning like an idiot, clapped his hands together.**  

“See?” he said. “**Norwegians. Badass.**”  

And with that, the sleigh **rolled into the Ice Hotel’s entrance, where the real madness was about to begin.**  

---

###**Tromsø – The Ice Hotel & The Price Jeff Won’t Admit To**

The **sleigh glided** past a **small, recessed wooden building**, its windows glowing faintly with warm light against the otherwise frozen landscape.  

Jeff **gestured toward it**, catching Naomi’s attention.  

“That’s the sauna,” he said. “They do it **twice a day**—once in the morning to start the day, once in the evening to end the workday.”  

Naomi, **still reeling from everything she had experienced in the last two hours**, looked at him, then at the sauna, then back at him.  

“…That actually sounds *really* nice.”  

Jeff **smirked.** “Yeah. You’ll love it.”  

Then, **before she could ask another 300 questions about sauna culture, the sleigh rolled up to their destination.**  

And suddenly—**they were there.**  

The **Ice Hotel.**  

### **Inside the Ice Hotel**  

Stepping through the entrance felt like **entering another world.**  

The moment they crossed the threshold, **the air changed.**  

It wasn’t *warm,* exactly. But it also wasn’t **the biting, skin-freezing cold they expected.**  

Naomi, **bundled up and still half-processing reality, glanced around.**  

“Okay, weird question,” she said. “Why is it… *kind of warm in here?*”  

Jodi, **also inspecting the surroundings, muttered,** “Yeah, what the hell?”  

Jeff **grinned**, clearly expecting this reaction.  

“The ice insulates the place,” he said. “Blocks the wind, traps the heat inside. So even though it’s freezing, it doesn’t feel as bad as it does outside.”  

Naomi **slowly nodded, accepting this new Arctic wizardry as fact.**  

Then, as she **looked at the walls**, she suddenly **froze.**  

“…Um.”  

Tara, **taking in the same sight, squinted.**  

“Okay. **What. The. Hell.**”  

Because the **walls?**  

They were **almost completely transparent.**  

Naomi, **pointing at the nearest one, whispered,** “You can *see through it.*”  

Jodi, **ever the pedantic one, corrected her immediately.**  

“The word you’re looking for is **translucent.**”  

Naomi **turned sharply.** “HOW CAN YOU THINK ABOUT GRAMMAR AT A TIME LIKE THIS?!”  

Jodi **shrugged.** “I contain multitudes.”  

Tara, **pressing a gloved hand against the nearest ice wall, exhaled slowly.**  

“…This is **unreal.**”  

Jeff, **grinning, nodded toward the reception area.**  

“Oh, it gets better,” he said. “**We rented the entire hotel.**”  

Jodi **snapped her head toward him.**  

Tara and Naomi **whipped around.**  

“…**WHAT?**”  

Jeff, **already prepared for this reaction, held up a hand.** “Okay, *before* you freak out—”  

“**TOO LATE, BUDDY,**” Tara **cut in.**  

“—it only has **14 rooms.**”  

The room fell silent.  

Then:  

Jodi **blinked.**  

Naomi, **baffled, whispered,** “That’s it?”  

Jeff **nodded.** “That’s it.”  

Tara **rubbed her temples.** “I was *ready* to call you an insane rich bastard, but…” she paused, then finally muttered, “Okay. That’s less *psychotic* than I was expecting.”  

Jodi **narrowed her eyes.**  

“…And *how much* does a room cost per night?”  

Jeff **immediately shook his head.** “Babe, you don’t want to know.”  

Jodi crossed her arms. “**I do, though.**”  

Jeff smirked. “No, because you’d hold that against me.”  

Jodi, **without hesitation, deadpanned,** “**You are correct.**”  

Tara **burst out laughing.**  

Naomi, **still touching the walls like she was trying to understand how this was real, muttered,** “I cannot get over how **dreamy** things look through the ice.”  

Jodi, **finally appreciating it, exhaled.**  

“…Yeah. It’s **insane.**”  

She pressed her hand against a **thick, three-foot block of solid ice** and looked through it—**everything was distorted, glowing, frozen in time.**  

It was **unreal.**  

Tara, **still staring at the walls, shook her head.**  

“This place is actually kinda **magical.**”  

Jeff, **smug as hell, slung an arm around Jodi’s shoulders.**  

“You’re welcome.”  

Jodi **rolled her eyes.** “I *hate* how much you love being right.”  

Jeff **kissed her temple.** “*Nah.* You love it.”  

And, honestly?  

**She kinda did.**  

---

### **Tromsø – The $4,000 Ice Bed & An Unexpected Guest**  

The **bar was glowing.**  

Not in a metaphorical way—**it was literally glowing.**  

Soft, golden lights **shimmered through the ice**, casting **dreamy reflections everywhere**, making it feel like they had stepped into **some kind of surreal, frozen dreamscape.**  

And as soon as they entered, Jeff—**far too casual for the nonsense he was about to drop—** finally answered Jodi’s earlier question.  

“Oh, by the way,” he said, **grabbing a drink menu**, “**a room here costs about $4,000 a night.**”  

Jodi **froze mid-step.**  

Tara and Naomi **immediately turned to him.**  

“**I’m sorry, WHAT?**” Jodi snapped.  

Jeff **shrugged.** “They throw in a lot of stuff in the deal.”  

Jodi’s **brain short-circuited.** “WHAT **STUFF?!** A **fucking ice sculpture of my first pet?!**”  

Jeff **grinned.** “Relax, babe. It’s mostly private tours, dinner, guided Northern Lights viewings, all that.”  

Jodi **rubbed her temples.** “**FOUR. THOUSAND. DOLLARS. A NIGHT.**”  

Tara, **laughing into her scarf, muttered,** “I love how *this* is what finally breaks you.”  

Naomi, **staring at the bar, whispered,** “Oh my god.”  

Jodi, **still spiraling about the room price, turned to her.** “I KNOW, RIGHT? IT’S INSANE.”  

Naomi, **still whispering, shook her head.** “No. *Not that.*”  

Jodi **followed Naomi’s gaze.**  

And **froze completely.**  

Because sitting at the **bar, sipping a cocktail, looking utterly unbothered by her own existence—**  

Was **Charla.**  

Jeremy’s wife.  

A woman who **almost never** traveled outside Ohio to see her husband.  

And yet, **here she was, in an Ice Hotel in the literal Arctic.**  

Jodi **blinked.**  

Then blinked again.  

Then—**just to be sure—** whispered, “The fuck?”  

Tara, **clocking her at the same time, immediately turned to Jeff.**  

“Okay, what *is happening*?” she demanded. “Charla never fucking goes anywhere.”  

Jeff, **also looking confused as hell, muttered,** “I—yeah. That’s weird as shit.”  

Jodi, **still staring at Charla like she might be a mirage, whispered,** “Is she a *hologram*?”  

Naomi, **not knowing what to do with this information, slowly took a seat.**  

Meanwhile, Tara—**who had started counting famous people the second they walked in—**  

**Immediately counted four.**  

She leaned toward Jodi.  

“**Dude.** There are **four** famous people in this room *right now.*”  

Jodi, **eyes still locked on Charla, muttered,** “Yeah, well, thank **fuck** Avril’s not here.”  

Unfortunately, she **whispered that just a little too loudly.**  

A man at a nearby table—**who had clearly been listening in—**  

**snorted into his drink.**  

Jodi **immediately whipped around.**  

The man **grinned at her.**  

Jeff, **sipping his whiskey, casually muttered,**  

“Babe. **We’re being observed.**”  

Jodi **groaned, dragging a hand over her face.**  

**This night was already getting way too weird.**  

And she had a **strong feeling it was only going to get weirder.**  

---

### **Tromsø – Unexpected Reunion & Absolutely No Boundaries**  

Jodi, **still half-convinced Charla was a mirage,** took a deep breath and strode across the ice bar.  

“**Well, holy shit.**”  

Charla **looked up, blinked, and then grinned.**  

“**No fucking way!**”  

And just like that, the two women **immediately embraced**, laughing, hugging **way too hard for two people standing inside a hotel made of ice.**  

“It’s been **four years!**” Jodi said, **pulling back, shaking her head in disbelief.**  

Charla nodded. “Yeah, last time was—”  

Jodi **winced.** “Gilley’s funeral.”  

Both women went **quiet for a beat.**  

Then Charla **squeezed Jodi’s hand.**  

“Well, *this* is a way better setting.”  

Jodi **grinned.** “I’ll drink to that.”  

They both lifted their glasses—**to better times, to ridiculous Arctic reunions, to not being at a fucking funeral.**  

Charla, **smirking, took a slow sip,** then eyed Jodi.  

“So,” she said, **grinning**, “**you’re finally letting Jeff pay for some shit.**”  

Jodi **snorted.**  

“It’s for the **weekend only.**”  

Charla **raised an eyebrow.** “Uh-huh.”  

Jodi **grinned wickedly,** leaning in.  

“And I’m gonna spend a *lot* of time on my back, taking it off in sweat equity.”  

They **burst into laughter so loud that at least three people turned to look.**  

Tara, **watching from her seat, muttered,** “Jesus **Christ.**”  

Naomi, **who had just taken a sip of her cocktail, choked.**  

Jeff, **utterly unbothered, took another sip of whiskey.**  

Jodi, **still grinning, leaned on the bar.**  

“Alright, but **seriously—**why the *hell* are you here? You never travel.”  

Charla **sighed, setting her glass down.**  

“Well,” she said, “Jeremy asked me to fly over because he was struggling a little bit with his sobriety. So I dropped everything and came over that evening.”  

Jodi **blinked.**  

Then she softened a little.  

“…Oh.”  

Charla **shrugged, ever the no-nonsense type.** “Figured he needed me.”  

Jodi **nodded.** “Yeah, I get that.”  

Charla **paused.**  

Then smirked.  

“But *if I’m being real*?” she added, lowering her voice.  

“I suspect he just wanted to get laid.”  

**Right on cue—**  

“**That is 100% correct.**”  

Jeremy **walked up behind her, sliding into the conversation like he had been waiting for his cue.**  

Jodi, **watching Charla fight back a laugh, turned to Jeremy.**  

“You absolute *piece of shit.*”  

Jeremy, **grinning unapologetically, threw an arm around his wife’s shoulders.**  

“I mean,” he said, **grinning at Charla,** “**I do need you.**”  

Charla **rolled her eyes.** “Uh-huh.”  

Jodi **shook her head, laughing.**  

Tara, **watching all of this unfold, leaned toward Jeff.**  

“Your family is so **fucking weird.**”  

Jeff, **not even slightly offended, nodded.**  

“Yeah. **I know.**”  

Naomi, **still trying to process all of this, whispered,** “Is *everyone* in your orbit just *chronically unfiltered?*”  

Jodi **grinned.** “You love it.”  

Naomi, **deadpan, took a sip of her drink.**  

“I *fear* it.”  

And with that, the **party in the Ice Hotel got just a little more ridiculous.**

### **Tromsø – The Legend of Gilley: Hollywood’s Favorite Mistake**  

Jodi had **exactly three seconds** of peace before Jeremy and Jeff—**fully reunited, fully fueled by whiskey, and fully in their element—**  

**Started reminiscing about the absolute madness that was their best friend, Gilley.**  

“Man,” Jeremy said, shaking his head with a **nostalgic smirk**, “we’re sitting in a fucking hotel made of ice, but it still doesn’t feel half as surreal as some of the *shit* that guy pulled.”  

Jeff **grinned into his drink.** “Absolute legend.”  

Tara, **already intrigued, leaned forward.** “Alright, hold up. I feel like I *know* the name, but I don’t have the details.”  

Jeremy **snorted.** “That’s because the details were usually **not safe for public consumption.**”  

Naomi, **curious but terrified, hesitated.** “Should we be hearing this?”  

Jeff **shrugged.** “Probably not.”  

Jodi, **sipping her drink, deadpanned,** “Oh, *definitely* not.”  

Jeremy, **fully committed now, launched into it.**  

**“Okay. So. Picture this.”**  

He gestured broadly, as if **setting the stage for the most ridiculous shit imaginable.**  

“Gilley. Drunk as *hell* in L.A., standing on a hotel rooftop, wearing nothing but a towel and a pair of sunglasses—**and somehow, in the span of two months, he managed to fuck half of Hollywood.**”  

Naomi **nearly dropped her glass.** “*WHAT?!*”  

Tara **perked up immediately.** “Oh, *I am listening.*”  

Jeff **leaned back, smirking.** “And not just *any* half.”  

Jeremy **grinned.** “Nope. He somehow bagged **the most famous, most unattainable, most *certified A-list* women in the industry.**”  

Jodi **rolled her eyes but was still smiling.** “And he *never* shut up about it.”  

“*Never,*” Jeff confirmed.  

Jeremy, **clearly getting way too into it, started counting on his fingers.**  

“Okay, so off the top of my head—**one Oscar winner, three nominees, two supermodels, and at least one literal princess.**”  

Naomi **choked.** “A *princess?!*”  

Tara, **cackling, slammed her glass down.** “*WHO WAS HE?!*”  

Jeff, **smirking, took another drink.** “Just some dipshit from Ohio.”  

Jeremy nodded, **grinning.** “Yeah, imagine a guy who looks like he should’ve been bartending at a shitty dive bar, except he had the confidence of a man who thought he was *destined to rule the Earth.*”  

Jodi **laughed, shaking her head.** “It worked for him *way too often.*”  

Jeremy, **still counting on his fingers, continued.** “Anyway, *on record,* he fucked at least three of the women on that year’s ‘Most Beautiful People’ list.”  

Tara, **staring at them in pure disbelief, whispered,** “That’s **not even real.**”  

Naomi, **processing, muttered,** “He was like—like a *walking cheat code.*”  

Jeremy **laughed.** “Yup. And the thing is, he never *chased* them. He just—*existed* in the right place, at the right time, with the right amount of ‘I don’t give a fuck.’”  

Jeff **nodded.** “Yeah, Gilley didn’t have game. He just had *energy.*”  

Jodi **grinned.** “Big *‘you’ll regret it if you don’t’* energy.”  

Tara, **still stunned, took a long sip.** “So what you’re telling me is… he pulled off the *impossible* *constantly.*”  

Jeremy **grinned.** “*Repeatedly.*”  

Naomi, **quiet for a long moment, finally whispered,**  

“…And yet *he’s dead* and I’m *alive?*”  

The table went **silent.**  

Then—  

Jodi **snorted so hard she nearly fell off her stool.**  

Jeremy and Jeff **immediately burst out laughing.**  

Tara **threw her head back, wheezing.**  

Naomi, **realizing what she just said, covered her face.**  

“I **DIDN’T MEAN IT LIKE THAT!**”  

Jodi, **crying with laughter, wiped her eyes.** “No, babe, *that was perfect.*”  

Jeff, **still grinning, raised his glass.** “To Gilley.”  

The others lifted their glasses in agreement.  

Because **if anyone deserved a toast in a ridiculous ice bar in the Arctic, it was that chaotic bastard.**  

**Wherever he was, he was definitely balls-deep in trouble.**

### **Tromsø – The 2014 Grammy Chase & The Gilley-Gwyneth Incident**  

Jeremy, **already laughing before he even started the story**, leaned forward on the bar. 

“Okay, okay, so, **2014 Grammys.** We had *just* performed, right?”  

Jeff, **grinning, nodded.** “Yeah, right after our set, Gilley fucking *disappeared.*”  

Tara, **intrigued, took a sip of her drink.** “How do you *lose* a grown man at the Grammys?”  

Jeremy threw up his hands. “It’s *Gilley.* He just *vanished.* One second he’s backstage, the next? *Gone.*”  

Jeff shook his head, smirking. “So we spent the next **fifteen fucking minutes** running through the depths of the Staples Center, looking for him.”  

Naomi, **wide-eyed, whispered,** “Like *a manhunt?*”  

Jeremy nodded. “*Exactly.* But here’s the part that **sticks out** the most for me.”  

Jeff **groaned** and gestured at himself. “Yeah. Because *this asshole* kicked me in the **balls** during the chase.”  

Jodi **blinked.** “Wait. **What?**”  

Tara **perked up immediately.** “Oh, I *need* to hear this.”  

Jeremy, **laughing, turned to Jeff.** “Dude, I sure as fuck mean to! You cut me with your antics, and I was running full speed—”  

“—And your fucking *knee* hit me so hard that I saw *actual fucking stars, Jer!*”  

Naomi **made a horrified noise.** “Oh my *God.*”  

Tara, **wheezing, pointed at Jeff.** “Wait, wait. So you got fucking **dropped** by Jeremy mid-chase?”  

Jeff, **dead serious, nodded.** “Dropped *instantly.* I was on my fucking knees. I thought my life was over.”  

Jodi, **grinning, patted Jeff’s thigh.** “Babe, you’ve had a *rough life.*”  

Jeff sighed. “You have *no idea.*”  

Jeremy, **still laughing, shook his head.** “Anyway, so Jeff is *down,* but we keep running. And then—”  

He held up a finger.  

“—we run into **Shaq.**”  

Naomi **gasped.** “*THE Shaq?*”  

Jeff nodded. “**Seven-foot-one, actual Shaquille O’Neal.**”  

Tara slammed her drink down. “**WHAT THE FUCK.**”  

Jeremy, **grinning like a lunatic, continued.** “So Shaq sees us **frantically looking around**, and he *knows.* He fucking *knows* what’s up.”  

Jeff, **nodding, took over.**  

“And he just points toward a Papa John’s storage room and goes, ‘**Your boy just ran in there.**’”  

Naomi **blinked slowly.**  

“…Why was there a **Papa John’s storage room** at the Grammys?”  

Jeremy threw up his hands. “It’s *America,* Naomi. Shit just *exists* sometimes.”  

Jodi, **laughing, waved a hand.** “Whatever, whatever—so what happened next?”  

Jeff **sighed dramatically.** “We hear people having **very, very obvious sex.**”  

Naomi, **already regretting her life choices, covered her face.**  

Tara **grinned.** “So, obviously, you barged in.”  

Jeremy **pointed at her.** “Damn **right** we did.”  

Jodi **winced.** “Oh, *no.*”  

Jeff **sighed deeply.** “And *there he was.*”  

Jeremy **grinned like a man who had seen the gates of hell and survived.**  

“**The G-Man.**”  

Jeff, **nodding grimly, finished the sentence.**  

“**Railing Gwyneth Paltrow.**”  

The **bar went dead silent.**  

Tara **threw her hands up.** “**NO THE FUCK HE WASN’T.**”  

Naomi, **absolutely blue-screened, whispered,** “*THE Gwyneth Paltrow?!*”  

Jeff, **nodding, took another drink.** “Yeah. Gilley was **balls deep** in an Oscar winner.”  

Jeremy, **grinning, pointed at Jodi.** “And that’s the *Oscar winner* I was talking about earlier.”  

Jodi, **facepalming, muttered,** “Of *course* it was.”  

Then—**before Jeremy could keep going—**  

He suddenly turned to Jeff, **smirking.**  

“And speaking of which,” he said, **grinning too wide**, “**Jeff knows a thing or two about what an Oscar winner tastes like.**”  

Jodi **immediately snapped her head up.**  

Jeremy wiggled his eyebrows **like the worst person alive.**  

Jeff, **unbothered, just smirked.**  

Naomi, **blinking rapidly, whispered,** “Oh hell, *wait, we just—*”  

And Jodi, **before this could turn into *an actual fucking circus,*** held up her hands.  

“Okay, **first of all—**”  

She took a breath, **looked Jeremy dead in the eye, and said:**  

“I already **talked to Kirsten.**”  

Jeremy **froze.**  

Tara **blinked.**  

Naomi, **mouth slightly open, whispered,** “Oh **shit.**”  

Jeremy, **realizing his mistake, cleared his throat.**  

“Well,” he muttered, suddenly looking down at his drink. “Alright then.”  

Jodi **smirked.** “Yeah. That’s what I thought.”  

Jeff, **leaning into Jodi with a smug grin, whispered,** “*Handled it, huh?*”  

Jodi **tilted her head toward him.**  

“*Handled it.*”  

Jeremy, **sufficiently put in his place, lifted his glass.** “Fair enough.”  

Tara, **still overwhelmed by *everything***, took a deep breath.  

“Alright,” she muttered, **rubbing her temples.**  

“I need *so much more alcohol* to deal with this fucking group.”  

Jeff **grinned.** “Welcome to the club.”

### **Tromsø – The Truth About Gilley, the Goat-Getting, & Jeff’s First Time**  

Tara, **arms crossed, already a few drinks deep,** leaned forward.

“All **chaos sex,**” Jeff confirmed.

Then:  

“**Kathy.**”

Jeff **visibly stiffened.**

Jeff **exhaled sharply, his jaw tightening.** “That’s **pretty damn disrespectful.**”

Jeff **narrowed his eyes.** “Yeah.”  

And then—**with the ease of someone who knew *exactly* how to press his brother’s buttons—**  

Jeremy smirked and said, **“Lick my balls and die.”**  

Jeff **immediately cracked up.**  

Jeremy **started laughing.**  

Jodi, **watching this with *deep* confusion, frowned.** “**What the fuck just happened?**”  

Tara **held up a finger.** “Wait. No. We do *not* move on from that. What *just* occurred?”

Jeremy nodded. “Yeah, if we get into it, you’re gonna regret asking.”  

Naomi, **dead serious, whispered,** “I *already* regret it.”  

Tara, **shaking her head, muttered,** “This is the **weirdest fucking family.**”  

Jodi, **still smirking, took a sip of her drink.**  

And then—**because she was genuinely curious now—** she asked:  

“Actually, serious question.”  

She tilted her head, watching Jeff carefully.  

“When did *you* and Kathy first become intimate?”  

The table went **silent.**  

Jeff **immediately looked uncomfortable.**  

Which—**for Jodi—was *hilarious.***  

Jim, the band’s drummer—**who had been quiet this whole time, sipping his beer like an old man watching chaos unfold—**  

**Casually chimed in.**  

“Oh, Kathy was *pissed* she wasn’t his first.”  

Jodi **blinked.** “Wait. *What?*”  

Jim nodded, **grinning slightly.** “Yeah. And because Jeff was clueless and a *bozo*, he lost out for *months*.”  

Jodi **grinned wickedly.**  

Jeff **exhaled sharply.** “Jesus **Christ**, Jim.”  

Jodi, **delighted, turned fully to Jeff.**  

“**Wait.** You’re telling me Kathy was actually **jealous** that she wasn’t the first?”  

Jeff, **clearly still uncomfortable, muttered,** “**Mox nix.**”  

Jodi, **watching him closely, tilted her head.** “Oh, does it *not* matter, babe?”  

Jeff took a slow sip of his whiskey.  

“Not at all.”  

Jodi **smirked.**  

“Bullshit. **It still bothers you.**”  

Jeff **rolled his eyes.** “It was **thirty-three years ago.**”  

Jodi, **leaning in slightly, grinned.** “And yet **you’re still thinking about it.**”  

Jeremy, **laughing his ass off, leaned on the bar.** “Dude, she *got* you.”  

Jeff, **exhaling slowly, took another drink.** “This entire conversation is fucking **stupid.**”  

Jim **shrugged.** “I don’t know. I think it’s **hilarious.**”  

Tara, **grinning, clinked her glass against Jim’s.** “I *like* you.”  

Jeff, **visibly done, muttered,** “**I hate all of you.**”  

Jodi **laughed, kissed his cheek, and whispered,** “*No, you don’t.*”  

And Jeff—**still grumbling, still clearly annoyed, still *bothered* after thirty-three fucking years—**  

**Said nothing.**  

Because Jodi was **right.**

### **Tromsø – The Avril Conversation That Jeff Can't Escape**

In fact—he looked **a little too calm.**  

Which, for Jodi, was **a giant red flag.**

“Oh, she’s **great.**”

Jeff nodded back, **settling in like he was about to give a goddamn TED Talk.**

Tara, **now deeply invested in this psychological experiment, leaned forward.**

Naomi **blinked rapidly.**  

Tara, **staring at them, pointed at Jeff.** “I—*what the fuck is happening inside your brain right now?*”  

Naomi, **visibly distressed, whispered,** “Why are men *like this?*”  

Jodi, **tapping her fingers on the bar, stayed quiet, letting him keep going.**

Tara **blinked twice.**  

Naomi, **clutching her drink like a life raft, whispered,** “I am **so** out of my depth.”  

Jodi, **who had been quiet for way too long, finally scratched her head.**  

Because **something** was off.  

Jeff wasn’t **dodging.**  Wasn’t **getting defensive.**  Wasn’t **even annoyed.**  If anything—**he sounded *upbeat.***  Like he had **already made peace with whatever internal conflict he should’ve been having.**  

And that?  That **threw Jodi off.**  **Why the hell was he so… chill about this?**  

She narrowed her eyes.  

Jeff, **finally noticing her expression, smirked slightly.**  

“…What?”  

Jodi **sipped her drink slowly.**  Then, **tilting her head, watching him carefully, asked:**  **“Why are you so *weirdly okay* with all this?”**  

Jeff, **leaning on the bar, grinned.**  “I just am.”  

Jodi **did not like that answer.**  Not at all.  Something **wasn’t adding up.**  

Tara, **looking between them, muttered,** “What the *fuck* are we walking into here?”  

Naomi, **wide-eyed, whispered,** “It’s like they’re playing 4D chess and I don’t even know what game this is.”  

Jodi, **still watching Jeff closely, exhaled through her nose.**  **She’d get to the bottom of this.**  Because **something** was going on.  And she wasn’t **gonna let him off the hook that easy.**

“Oh, yeah,” he said. “**Everyone in the band is.**”
