Jeff nodded. “Yep.”  

Jim, **grinning, lifted his glass.** “Fucking *certifiable*, babe.”  

Jeremy, **stretching, nodded in agreement.** “Yeah, you kinda *have* to be.”  

Naomi, **still confused, muttered,** “Why?”  

Jeff, **taking a slow sip of whiskey, smirked.**  

“**Self-preservation mechanism.**”  

Tara and Naomi **exchanged a look.**  

Jodi, **smirking knowingly, leaned in.**  

“They’ve been doing this *too long*, babe,” she said. “You can’t be **normal** and survive in this industry. It’ll eat you alive.”  

Tara, **slowly nodding, exhaled sharply.**  

“…That *actually* makes sense.”  

Naomi, **still struggling, muttered,** “I don’t *want* it to make sense.”  

Jeff **grinned.** “Too bad.”  

Tara, **accepting her fate, picked up her glass and raised it.**  

“Well, fuck it. Here’s to being *bent as hell*.”  

Jim, Jeremy, Charla, Jeff, and Jodi **immediately clinked glasses.**  

Naomi, **slowly, reluctantly, lifted hers too.**  

“…I *fear* what I’ve gotten myself into.”  

Jodi **laughed, throwing an arm around her.**  

“Oh, babe. **You have *no* idea.**”  

And with that, Tara **officially stopped trying to understand it all.**  

Because some things?  

**Were just too fucking bent to question.**

### **Tromsø – The Bentest Thing This Band Has Ever Done**  

Tara, **leaning forward, arms crossed, fully committed to getting an answer**, demanded,  

“Alright, then. What’s the **most bent** thing this band has ever done?”  

Jim, **who had been casually sipping his drink like a man with *too many* stories**, set his glass down, exhaled, and **looked every single person in the eye.**  

“Alright,” he said, voice **low, serious,** like he was about to **reveal the nuclear codes.**  

“This does **not** leave this room.”  

The table **went silent.**  

Tara and Naomi **immediately leaned in.**  

Jodi, **already sensing some *bullshit* was about to drop, stayed completely still.**  

Jeff, **who had been relaxed up until now, noticeably **tensed.**  

Jim **exhaled sharply.**  

“Everyone in the band fucked Kathy at one point or another.”  

Jodi **blinked.**  

Tara, **mouth slightly open, just whispered,** “What.”  

Naomi, **fully stunned, just sat there.**  

Jim, **seeing their reactions, nodded.**  

“Oh yeah. **Every single one of us.**”  

Jodi **snorted, shaking her head.** “I *knew* it was bad, but *Jesus Christ*.”  

Jim held up a finger. “And not just the band. **The band manager. Our top lawyer. Two of the backup singers. And, quite possibly, the lovely Island Records VP you’ll all be meeting in the morning.**”  

Tara **was fully, physically malfunctioning.**  

Naomi, **head in her hands, whispered,** “*This is not my world.*”  

Jeremy, **grinning, leaned in.** “But the thing is—”  

Jim nodded, **cutting him off.** “The thing is, **we all learned the lesson after a few times.**”  

Jodi, **smirking, crossed her arms.** “Except *one* person.”  

Jim, **dead serious, turned to Jeff.**  

“**Jeffy never did.**”  

Jeff, **exhaling sharply, muttered,** “Jesus **Christ.**”  

Jim, **looking his bandmate dead in the eyes, didn’t blink.**  

“And I’m sorry to be this frank about it,” he continued, “but you **never learned** not to stick your dick in her.”  

The table **went silent.**  

Jodi **tried.**  

She *really* tried.  

But then she **burst out laughing.**  

Tara **gasped, covering her mouth.**  

Naomi, **completely short-circuited, whispered,** “*I am living in a lawless land.*”  

Jeff, **rubbing his temple, muttered,** “I fucking *hate* this conversation.”  

Jim, **raising his glass, smirked.** “You *should*.”  

Jeremy, **laughing, clapped his brother on the back.** “Dude, you *never* learned.”  

Jeff just **sat there, suffering.**  

Jodi, **grinning, leaned into him.**  

“Babe,” she whispered, **smug as hell.**  

Jeff sighed. “*What.*”  

Jodi **smirked wider.**  

“**I learned.**”  

And that?  

That was the **final fucking straw.**  

The table **exploded into laughter.**  

Jeff just **dropped his head onto the bar.**  

And Jodi?  

Jodi just **kept fucking winning.**

### **Tromsø – The Truth Underneath It All**  

The **laughter died down.**  

The energy at the table **shifted**—not sharply, not immediately, but **enough** that Jodi noticed it.  

Jeff, **still nursing his whiskey, exhaled slowly.**  

“Listen,” he said, **his voice a little lower now.** “I *did* learn. The whole album—*Be First, Be Smarter or Cheat*—was one gigantic **goodbye letter** to everyone in our friend group.”  

Jeremy, **leaning on the bar, shook his head.**  

“We know,” he said. “We **know.**”  

Then, **without even looking up, he added—**  

“But you were a **fucking disgrace, dude.**”  

The table **went silent.**  

Jodi, **blinking, slowly turned to Jeremy.**  

Jeremy, **still calm, still casual**, picked up his drink, swirled it a little, then looked directly at his brother.  

“You *nearly butchered* your woman’s love,” he said, **voice steady, matter-of-fact.** “Coz you treated her like a *sidepiece* when you fucked Avril for a **year straight.**”  

Jodi, **stiffening slightly, exhaled through her nose.**  

Jeff, **jaw tightening, leaned forward.**  

“You *still* pissed over that?”  

Jeremy, **sighing, finally reached into his pocket and pulled out his eyeglasses.**  

He put them on, and for the **first time all night**, Jodi thought he looked **old.**  

Not just **older than Jeff**—**actually old.**  

Then, **calmly**, he said,  

“It’s **water under the bridge.**”  

He took a slow sip of his whiskey.  

“I neither **forgive** nor **forgot.**”  

Another sip.  

“I’m just **moving along.**”  

Jodi, **watching the exchange carefully, realized something.**  

She didn’t get **many opportunities** to interact with Jeff’s younger brother.  

And now?  

Now she was seeing that **their relationship was a lot more strained than Jeff had always claimed.**  

Because Jeff had always made it seem like things between them were just **brotherly banter**—like Jeremy just loved to give him shit for old mistakes.  

But this?  

This was **different.**  

Jeremy wasn’t **teasing.**  

He wasn’t **joking.**  

He was **stating a fact.**  

That he’d moved on.  

But that he **hadn’t let it go.**  

Jodi, **silent, just watched Jeff’s face.**  

Because for the **first time tonight**, Jeff wasn’t **smug, wasn’t deflecting, wasn’t brushing things off.**  

He just looked…  

**Tired.**  

And Jodi, **for once, just let the silence sit.**  

Because this?  

This was something **deeper.**  

Something **not even whiskey could fix.**  

---

### **Tromsø – Six Thoughts & One Reality Check**  

The **room was surreal.**  

The walls—**translucent, thick blocks of ice**—distorted everything in a **dreamy, otherworldly way.**  

Their breath came in soft clouds, the cold **seeping into everything,** yet somehow, it wasn’t uncomfortable.  

Jodi, **still absorbing the night, sat on the frozen edge of the ice bed, watching Jeff.**  

He was standing there, **still in his coat, hands in his pockets, clearly lost in thought.**  

So she just **asked.**  

**“What’s going on in your head?”**  

Jeff, **snorting softly, exhaled through his nose.**  

“I’ve got **six different thoughts** in my head right now.”  

Jodi **tilted her head.**  

“Oh? Pick one.”  

Jeff smirked, **but it didn’t quite reach his eyes.**  

“Which one do you want?”  

He held up a finger. **“How I want to fuck you.”**  

A second finger. **“How I want to kick my brother’s ass.”**  

A third. **“What a fun time I’m having.”**  

A fourth. **“Why I’m looking forward to tomorrow’s recording session.”**  

A fifth. **“Why I’m so damn bothered that no one ever leaves me alone, comfortable with my memories.”**  

And then, finally, a sixth.  

**“Or how I’m crushed that no one acknowledges my fucking *growth*—that I took up the Avril dare and didn’t partake of her flesh.”**  

Jodi, **watching him carefully, exhaled slowly.**  

“Babe,” she said, **calm, steady.**  

He looked at her.  

“You know I’m used to you going **six different ways at once.**”  

Jeff smirked, **but it was tired.**  

Jodi stood up, stepping closer, placing a **warm hand on his chest.**  

“But you need to **integrate your thinking,**” she said, voice soft but firm. “And you need to talk to me.”  

Jeff, **exhaling sharply, shook his head slightly.**  

“…It’s all just *there,*” he muttered. “All at once. All the time.”  

Jodi **nodded.**  

“I know.”  

And she did.  

Because this was **always** how it was with Jeff—**his brain didn’t work in single tracks.**  

It was always **six things at once.**  

But this?  

This was heavier.  

This was **all tangled up.**  

So she waited.  

Let him **sort it out.**  

Because Jeff **always had to sort it out before he could speak.**  

And finally—after a long moment—he did.  

---

### **Tromsø – First Things First**  

Jodi **watched him carefully**, felt the tension in his chest beneath her hand, saw the way his jaw was still **tight with too many thoughts.**  

So she made a decision.  

A very, **very** easy decision.  

She **tilted her head, smirking slightly.**  

“Alright,” she said. “Just this *once*—”  

Jeff **narrowed his eyes.** “Oh?”  

“—I’m gonna **suck you off first.**”  

Jeff **blinked.**  

Jodi **grinned.** “And *then* we’ll talk.”  

Jeff **snorted, shaking his head.** “That’s your plan?”  

Jodi **shrugged, stepping back toward the bed, already unzipping her coat.**  

“Yep. Because I expect that after I **empty your balls,** you’re gonna get your head straight.”  

Jeff, **watching her, finally cracked a real smile.**  

“…That so?”  

Jodi, **sitting on the edge of the ice bed, looking up at him, grinned wider.**  

“**Fucking right.**”  

And Jeff?  

Jeff, for the **first time in hours**, stopped **thinking about six things at once.**  

Because suddenly, he only had **one.**  

---

### **Tromsø – Jodi Knows Best**  

The **air was different now.**

She **tilted her head, smirking.**  

“So,” she said, **voice dripping with satisfaction.**  

“What did we learn?”

“That you,” he muttered, **running a hand over his face,** “always know what’s best for me.”  

Jodi **grinned wider.**  

“Thank *God,*” Jeff added.

Jodi **grinned, resting her chin in her hand.** “We’ll see.”

For the first time that night?  

He wasn’t thinking about **six things at once.**  

Just **her.**  

And **fuck,** was that a relief.

### **Tromsø – One Voice, One Chorus**  

Jodi **knelt in front of him, slow and deliberate, her hands sliding up his thighs.**

A **release.**

They all **disappeared.**  

Because **Jodi had him now.**  

And **nothing else mattered.**

### **Tromsø – No Thinking, Just Feeling**

She was **putting him back together.**

### **Tromsø – The Aftermath & The Order to Talk**  

Jodi, **still on her knees, still smirking, made a whole show of swallowing.**  

A **deliberate, slow, performative swallow.**

But no.  

Because **as soon as she wiped her mouth, she sat back on her heels, looked him dead in the eye, and said—**  

**“Alright. Now talk.”**

And then—**in that soft, firm voice that brooked absolutely no argument—**

**“Talk.”**

Now, it was time to **face it.**

### **Tromsø – Jeff Lays It All Out**

So he just **spoke.**  

**“Alright.”**  

Jodi, **still kneeling in front of him, nodded.**  

Jeff leaned forward, elbows on his knees. **“You want it all?”**  

Jodi, **calm but firm, met his eyes.**  

**“All of it.”**  

Jeff **exhaled sharply, then started.**  

### **Jeremy**  

“**Jeremy still resents me.**”  

Jodi **stayed quiet, letting him go at his own pace.**  

Jeff **sighed.**  

“He’s *always* gonna resent me for the Avril year. Not just because I was fucking her, but because—” he gestured vaguely, “—I *let it happen* in a way that fucked up everything around me. He saw it as **disrespecting the band, disrespecting you, just being a self-absorbed asshole.**”  

Jodi **tilted her head.** “And?”  

Jeff **let out a low laugh, shaking his head.**  

“And **he’s not wrong.**”  

Jodi’s **eyebrow lifted slightly, but she still said nothing.**  

Jeff sighed, rubbing his hands together.  

“He doesn’t *hate* me for it anymore. But he’s never gonna be fully past it. That’s why he said **‘I neither forgive nor forgot.’**”  

Jodi, **watching him closely, nodded.**  

Yeah.  

She had **felt that.**  

Jeremy’s **tone, his body language—it wasn’t just brotherly ribbing.** It was **a fracture that never fully healed.**  

### **Avril**  

Jeff **rolled his shoulders, shifting gears.**  

“**Avril’s a whole other thing.**”  

Jodi **snorted.** “Obviously.”  

Jeff **shot her a look.** “I know what it *seems* like.”  

Jodi **arched a brow.** “Do you?”  

Jeff smirked. “Yeah. It seems like I’m just **keeping a desperate woman on a leash.**”  

Jodi **shrugged.** “Kinda does, babe.”  

Jeff sighed.  

“It’s not that. It’s that…” He ran a hand through his hair again. “**I needed to see if I could actually just *work* with her.** No complications. No distractions.”  

Jodi, **arms crossed, nodded slowly.** “And?”  

Jeff **exhaled, shaking his head.**  

“She’s a *pro* when she’s working. But when we’re not?” He gestured vaguely. “She *tries*.”  
Jodi **smirked.** “Hard.”  

Jeff **sighed.** “*So hard.*”  

Jodi **tilted her head.** “And?”  

Jeff finally **looked her in the eye.**  

“And **I don’t fucking bite.**”  

Jodi **didn’t look away.**  

She could see it.  

**He meant that.**  

He wasn’t **dodging, wasn’t making excuses, wasn’t downplaying it.**  

He was **actually telling the truth.**  

Jeff ran his fingers over his mouth.  

“That’s what kills me. Nobody **acknowledges** that. Nobody says, ‘Hey, maybe Jeff’s actually **grown up.** Maybe he’s not just gonna fuck a problem away anymore.’”  

Jodi, **watching him, exhaled slowly.**  

Yeah.  

She saw it now.  

He wasn’t **upbeat about Avril because he wanted her.**  

He was upbeat because **he knew he wasn’t going to make the same mistake again.**  

### **The Past & The Weight of It**  

Jeff leaned back, rubbing his jaw.  

“And the rest?” he muttered. “The **memories?** The fact that nobody ever lets me be *comfortable* with them?”  

He sighed.  

“I don’t get to have *just* memories. I get to have **commentary.** From *everyone.* Constantly.”  

Jodi, **nodding slowly, muttered,** “Yeah. I saw that tonight.”

“I live my life. **Other people narrate it.**”  

Jodi, **watching him carefully, reached forward, rested a hand on his knee.**  

Jeff **glanced down at it, then back at her.**  

Jodi **exhaled, steady.**  

“**I hear you.**”  

Jeff **let out a breath he didn’t realize he was holding.**  

Jodi **squeezed his knee.**  

“And I get it.”

Because she **did.**  

She **actually fucking got it.**

### **Tromsø – Only Halfway There**

“Babe.”

Yeah, he **should’ve known she wasn’t gonna let him off that easy.**

### **Tromsø – The Other Three Thoughts**  

Jeff **chuckled softly, shaking his head.**  

“You probably always deserve answers.”  

Jodi **smirked, squeezing his knee again.** “*Probably?*”

“Fine. **Definitely.**”

“I’m actually *really* looking forward to tomorrow’s recording session.”  

Jodi tilted her head. “Because?”

This?  

This was **why he still did it.**  

**Not** for the fame.  
**Not** for the money.  
**Not** for the power.  

But because **he still fucking loved it.**  

Jodi **smiled.** “That’s the guy I love.”  

Jeff, **grinning now, nudged her playfully.** “Yeah, yeah.”  

### **Jeremy & the Last Unfinished Thing**  

And then—**his grin faded slightly.**  

Jodi **saw it immediately.**  

“Jeremy.”  

Jeff sighed. “Jeremy.”  

Jodi **stayed quiet, letting him sort his words.**

Jodi **softened a little, resting a hand on his thigh.**

“I know I fucked up,” he admitted. “I *own* it. I just wish he’d let me **move past it with him.**”  

Jodi, **squeezing his leg, nodded.**

The **Avril bullshit** didn’t matter.

The **memories** were what they were.  

But **Jeremy?**  

Jeremy was his **brother.**  

And Jeff still didn’t know if he’d **ever fully get him back.**  

Jodi, **exhaling softly, met his eyes.**  

“You done?”  

Jeff **sighed.**  

“…Yeah.”  

Jodi **tilted her head.**  

“How do you feel?”  

Jeff **thought about it.**  

And—**for the first time tonight—**  

He actually felt **lighter.**  

“…Better.”  

Jodi, **grinning, climbed up onto the ice bed beside him.**  

“Good,” she murmured, pressing a kiss to his temple. “That’s what I wanted.”  

Jeff, **relaxing into her touch, muttered,**  

“**I know.**”  

And finally—**finally—**his mind was quiet.  

For now.

### **Tromsø – Deep in the Studio & The Art of the Perfect Intro**  

The control room was **dimly lit, warm, and absolutely humming with intensity.**  

Jodi, Tara, and Naomi **slipped on headsets**, watching through the thick glass as the **band worked.**  

And by “worked,” it meant **playing the first ten seconds of a song over and over again.**  

Take after take.

Stopping. Talking. Trying again.  

**Perfecting it.**  

Jodi, **watching with fascination, leaned in slightly.**  

Jeff’s **bass playing was completely different in here.**  

**Live,** he was **fluid, wild, effortless.**  
**Here?** He was **precise, methodical, stripped down.**  

She almost **never** saw him record in the studio.  

And now that she was watching, she was **fucking intrigued.**  

Tara, **eyes flicking over all the equipment, exhaled sharply.**  

“Jesus. **I quit counting microphones at twenty.**”  

Tim, **grinning, leaned against the console.**  

“Yeah, we’ve got about **ninety.**”  

Tara, **blinking, turned to him.**  

“**Ninety?**”  

Tim **shrugged.** “Yeah. **Two drum kits,** five musicians, isolation booths—**it adds up.**”  

Naomi, **shaking her head, muttered,**  

“**This is so much more complicated than I thought.**”  

Jodi, **still listening, furrowed her brows.**  

“This is…**way heavier** than the stuff I’m used to from them.”  

Tim, **nodding, smirked.**  

“That’s on **Avril.**”  

Jodi, **blinking, turned to him.**  

“Yeah?”  

Tim **grinned, gesturing toward the window.**  

“**She’s playing electric guitar, too, while delivering the lyrics.**”  

Jodi **tilted her head, processing.**

There was an **extra layer of weight** to the mix. A **depth** she wasn’t used to.

Tim, **grinning wider, pointed toward the band.**

He stretched his arms behind his head.

Jodi, **watching Jeff shake his head and cue them up for yet another take, smirked.**  

“Oh, babe. **This might take a while.**”
